meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Tutorial)
content.seo_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade features.
content.answer_block: Transform your blank portfolio into a high-value asset by building a dynamic, tier-based SaaS pricing engine. By integrating Stripe API with intelligent rate-limiting, you demonstrate architectural maturity. This tutorial guides you through engineering a scalable billing system, proving to employers you can handle the complex logic behind real-world revenue streams.
content.intro:
The silence of an empty GitHub repository is the loudest obstacle a junior developer faces. You’ve studied the theory, but without a production-grade project, your skills remain invisible to hiring managers who demand evidence of architectural competence. You need more than a generic CRUD app; you need a system that handles money, users, and logic.

We are going to bridge that gap by building a sophisticated SaaS pricing module. This isn't just about UI; it's about backend robustness, security, and the ability to manage subscription states. Using Antigravity and Claude Code, we will turn your lack of experience into a showcase of high-level engineering prowess that makes recruiters stop scrolling.

steps.title: Architecting the Subscription Schema
steps.description: Define the data structures for plans, features, and user entitlements to ensure a scalable billing foundation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database schema for a multi-tier SaaS pricing system. Include tables for plans (basic, pro, enterprise), features with usage quotas, and a subscription state machine.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create database migration files based on the generated schema'

steps.title: Implementing Stripe Webhook Handlers
steps.description: Develop secure handlers to process subscription events, ensuring your database stays in sync with Stripe's state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Node.js webhook handler for Stripe events (invoice.paid, customer.subscription.deleted). Include signature verification and idempotency logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'integrate stripe-webhook-service.ts into existing express routing'

steps.title: Building the Pricing Logic Middleware
steps.description: Create a middleware that checks user entitlement before executing protected API endpoints based on their current plan.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a TypeScript middleware function that checks if a user's subscription allows them to access a specific resource based on their plan's feature flags.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply entitlement-guard middleware to all api/v1/projects routes'

steps.title: Designing the Dynamic Pricing UI
steps.description: Construct a responsive pricing table that dynamically updates based on active feature toggles and selected billing cycles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React component for a pricing table using Tailwind CSS. The component should accept props for monthly/yearly toggle and highlight the 'Recommended' plan.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor pricing-table.tsx to handle state switching for annual/monthly billing'

steps.title: Stress Testing the Billing Flow
steps.description: Simulate subscription upgrades and downgrades to ensure the system handles edge cases without data loss.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test suite using Jest to simulate a user upgrading from a free tier to a pro tier, verifying that the database entitlement flags update correctly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'execute billing-lifecycle.test.ts and report on coverage gaps'

pro_tips: Always implement idempotency keys in your webhook handlers; network retries are the silent killer of billing accuracy.
Never store price information purely in the frontend; always validate the plan ID against your secure backend database.
Use feature flags to decouple your billing logic from your core application logic, allowing for instant plan adjustments.
Implement a grace period logic for failed payments to improve user retention before hard-locking accounts.
Document the 'Why' behind your database schema choices in your README; this is what senior interviewers look for first.
content.next_steps: Once the billing system is live, deploy it to a staging environment and record a 60-second video demo showing how an account is upgraded. Add this link to your resume as a 'Live SaaS Architecture' project.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-ready billing system that proves you can build complex, revenue-generating software. Don't stop here; deploy this to Vercel and make it the crown jewel of your portfolio. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
